Pattern\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-callbackexpression\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e{expression}\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#path-item-object\"\u003ePath Item Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA Path Item Object used to define a callback request and expected responses.  A \u003ca href=\"/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/main/examples/v3.0/callback-example.yaml\"\u003ecomplete example\u003c/a\u003e is available.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis object MAY be extended with \u003ca href=\"#specification-extensions\"\u003eSpecification Extensions\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eKey Expression\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-key-expression\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Key Expression\" href=\"#key-expression\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe key that identifies the \u003ca href=\"#path-item-object\"\u003ePath Item Object\u003c/a\u003e is a \u003ca href=\"#runtime-expressions\"\u003eruntime expression\u003c/a\u003e that can be evaluated in the context of a runtime HTTP request/response to identify the URL to be used for the callback request.\nA simple example might be \u003ccode\u003e$request.body#/url\u003c/code\u003e.\nHowever, using a \u003ca href=\"#runtime-expressions\"\u003eruntime expression\u003c/a\u003e the complete HTTP message can be accessed.\nThis includes accessing any part of a body that a JSON Pointer \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6901\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eRFC6901\u003c/a\u003e can reference.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFor example, given the following HTTP request:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-httpspec notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"POST /subscribe/myevent?queryUrl=http://clientdomain.com/stillrunning HTTP/1.1\nHost: example.org\nContent-Type: application/json\nContent-Length: 187\n\n{\n  \u0026quot;failedUrl\u0026quot; : \u0026quot;http://clientdomain.com/failed\u0026quot;,\n  \u0026quot;successUrls\u0026quot; : [\n    \u0026quot;http://clientdomain.com/fast\u0026quot;,\n    \u0026quot;http://clientdomain.com/medium\u0026quot;,\n    \u0026quot;http://clientdomain.com/slow\u0026quot;\n  ]\n}\n\n201 Created\nLocation: http://example.org/subscription/1\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003ePOST\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e /subscribe/myevent?queryUrl=http://clientdomain.com/stillrunning HTTP/1.1\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-v\"\u003eHost:\u003c/span\u003e example.org\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-v\"\u003eContent-Type:\u003c/span\u003e application/json\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-v\"\u003eContent-Length:\u003c/span\u003e 187\u003c/span\u003e\n\n{\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e\"failedUrl\"\u003c/span\u003e : \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003ehttp://clientdomain.com/failed\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e,\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e\"successUrls\"\u003c/span\u003e : [\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003ehttp://clientdomain.com/fast\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e,\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003ehttp://clientdomain.com/medium\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e,\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003ehttp://clientdomain.com/slow\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\n  ]\n}\n\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e201 Created\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-v\"\u003eLocation:\u003c/span\u003e http://example.org/subscription/1\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe following examples show how the various expressions evaluate, assuming the callback operation has a path parameter named \u003ccode\u003eeventType\u003c/code\u003e and a query parameter named \u003ccode\u003equeryUrl\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eExpression\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"left\"\u003eValue\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e$url\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"http://example.org/subscribe/myevent?queryUrl=http://clientdomain.com/stillrunning\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttp://example.org/subscribe/myevent?queryUrl=http://clientdomain.com/stillrunning\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e$method\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003ePOST\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e$request.path.eventType\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003emyevent\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e$request.query.queryUrl\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"http://clientdomain.com/stillrunning\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttp://clientdomain.com/stillrunning\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e$request.header.content-Type\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003eapplication/json\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e$request.body#/failedUrl\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"http://clientdomain.com/stillrunning\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttp://clientdomain.com/stillrunning\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e$request.body#/successUrls/2\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"http://clientdomain.com/medium\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttp://clientdomain.com/medium\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e$response.header.Location\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"http://example.org/subscription/1\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttp://example.org/subscription/1\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eCallback Object Example\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-callback-object-example\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Callback Object Example\" href=\"#callback-object-example\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe following example shows a callback to the URL specified by the \u003ccode\u003eid\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003eemail\u003c/code\u003e property in the request body.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-yaml notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"myWebhook:\n  'http://notificationServer.com?transactionId={$request.body#/id}\u0026amp;email={$request.body#/email}':\n    post:\n      requestBody:\n        description: Callback payload\n        content:\n          'application/json':\n            schema:\n              $ref: '#/components/schemas/SomePayload'\n      responses:\n        '200':\n          description: webhook successfully processed and no retries will be performed\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003emyWebhook\u003c/span\u003e:\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003ehttp://notificationServer.com?transactionId={$request.body#/id}\u0026amp;email={$request.body#/email}\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e:\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003epost\u003c/span\u003e:\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003erequestBody\u003c/span\u003e:\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003edescription\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003eCallback payload\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003econtent\u003c/span\u003e:\n          \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eapplication/json\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eschema\u003c/span\u003e:\n              \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e$ref\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e#/components/schemas/SomePayload\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eresponses\u003c/span\u003e:\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e200\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e:\n          \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003edescription\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003ewebhook successfully processed and no retries will be performed\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 Name\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-examplesummary\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003esummary\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eShort description for the example.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-exampledescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eLong description for the example. \u003ca href=\"http://spec.commonmark.org/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eCommonMark syntax\u003c/a\u003e MAY be used for rich text representation.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-examplevalue\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003evalue\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eEmbedded literal example. The \u003ccode\u003evalue\u003c/code\u003e field and \u003ccode\u003eexternalValue\u003c/code\u003e field are mutually exclusive. To represent examples of media types that cannot naturally represented in JSON or YAML, use a string value to contain the example, escaping where necessary.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-exampleexternalvalue\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eexternalValue\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA URL that points to the literal example. This provides the capability to reference examples that cannot easily be included in JSON or YAML documents.  The \u003ccode\u003evalue\u003c/code\u003e field and \u003ccode\u003eexternalValue\u003c/code\u003e field are mutually exclusive.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis object MAY be extended with \u003ca href=\"#specification-extensions\"\u003eSpecification Extensions\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIn all cases, the example value is expected to be compatible with the type schema\nof its associated value.  Tooling implementations MAY choose to\nvalidate compatibility automatically, and reject the example value(s) if incompatible.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eExample Object Example\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-example-object-example\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Example Object Example\" href=\"#example-object-example\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-yaml notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"# in a model\nschemas:\n  properties:\n    name:\n      type: string\n      examples:\n        name:\n          $ref: http://example.org/petapi-examples/openapi.json#/components/examples/name-example\n\n# in a request body:\n  requestBody:\n    content:\n      'application/json':\n        schema:\n          $ref: '#/components/schemas/Address'\n        examples:\n          foo:\n            summary: A foo example\n            value: {\u0026quot;foo\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;bar\u0026quot;}\n          bar:\n            summary: A bar example\n            value: {\u0026quot;bar\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;baz\u0026quot;}\n      'application/xml':\n        examples:\n          xmlExample:\n            summary: This is an example in XML\n            externalValue: 'http://example.org/examples/address-example.xml'\n      'text/plain':\n        examples:\n          textExample:\n            summary: This is a text example\n            externalValue: 'http://foo.bar/examples/address-example.txt'\n\n\n# in a parameter\n  parameters:\n    - name: 'zipCode'\n      in: 'query'\n      schema:\n        type: 'string'\n        format: 'zip-code'\n        examples:\n          zip-example:\n            $ref: '#/components/examples/zip-example'\n\n# in a response\n  responses:\n    '200':\n      description: your car appointment has been booked\n      content:\n        application/json:\n          schema:\n            $ref: '#/components/schemas/SuccessResponse'\n          examples:\n            confirmation-success:\n              $ref: '#/components/examples/confirmation-success'\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e in a model\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eschemas\u003c/span\u003e:\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eproperties\u003c/span\u003e:\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003ename\u003c/span\u003e:\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003etype\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003estring\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eexamples\u003c/span\u003e:\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003ename\u003c/span\u003e:\n          \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e$ref\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003ehttp://example.org/petapi-examples/openapi.json#/components/examples/name-example\u003c/span\u003e\n\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e in a request body:\u003c/span\u003e\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003erequestBody\u003c/span\u003e:\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003econtent\u003c/span\u003e:\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eapplication/json\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e:\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eschema\u003c/span\u003e:\n          \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e$ref\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e#/components/schemas/Address\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eexamples\u003c/span\u003e:\n          \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003efoo\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003esummary\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003eA foo example\u003c/span\u003e\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003evalue\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e{\"foo\": \"bar\"}\u003c/span\u003e\n          \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003ebar\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003esummary\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003eA bar example\u003c/span\u003e\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003evalue\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e{\"bar\": \"baz\"}\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eapplication/xml\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e:\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eexamples\u003c/span\u003e:\n          \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003exmlExample\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003esummary\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003eThis is an example in XML\u003c/span\u003e\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eexternalValue\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003ehttp://example.org/examples/address-example.xml\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003etext/plain\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e:\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eexamples\u003c/span\u003e:\n          \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003etextExample\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003esummary\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003eThis is a text example\u003c/span\u003e\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eexternalValue\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003ehttp://foo.bar/examples/address-example.txt\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\n\n\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e in a parameter\u003c/span\u003e\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eparameters\u003c/span\u003e:\n    - \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003ename\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003ezipCode\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003ein\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003equery\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eschema\u003c/span\u003e:\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003etype\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003estring\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eformat\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003ezip-code\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eexamples\u003c/span\u003e:\n          \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003ezip-example\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e$ref\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e#/components/examples/zip-example\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\n\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e in a response\u003c/span\u003e\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eresponses\u003c/span\u003e:\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e200\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e:\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003edescription\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003eyour car appointment has been booked\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003econtent\u003c/span\u003e:\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eapplication/json\u003c/span\u003e:\n          \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eschema\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e$ref\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e#/components/schemas/SuccessResponse\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\n          \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eexamples\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003econfirmation-success\u003c/span\u003e:\n              \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e$ref\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e#/components/examples/confirmation-success\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eLink Object\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-link-object\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Link Object\" href=\"#link-object\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe \u003ccode\u003eLink object\u003c/code\u003e represents a possible design-time link for a response.\nThe presence of a link does not guarantee the caller's ability to successfully invoke it, rather it provides a known relationship and traversal mechanism between responses and other operations.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eUnlike \u003cem\u003edynamic\u003c/em\u003e links (i.e. links provided \u003cstrong\u003ein\u003c/strong\u003e the response payload), the OAS linking mechanism does not require link information in the runtime response.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFor computing links, and providing instructions to execute them, a \u003ca href=\"#runtime-expressions\"\u003eruntime expression\u003c/a\u003e is used for accessing values in an operation and using them as parameters while invoking the linked operation.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eFixed Fields\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-fixed-fields-17\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Fixed Fields\" href=\"#fixed-fields-17\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eField Name\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-linkoperationref\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eoperationRef\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA relative or absolute reference to an OAS operation. This field is mutually exclusive of the \u003ccode\u003eoperationId\u003c/code\u003e field, and MUST point to an \u003ca href=\"#operation-object\"\u003eOperation Object\u003c/a\u003e. Relative \u003ccode\u003eoperationRef\u003c/code\u003e values MAY be used to locate an existing \u003ca href=\"#operation-object\"\u003eOperation Object\u003c/a\u003e in the OpenAPI definition.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-linkoperationid\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eoperationId\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe name of an \u003cem\u003eexisting\u003c/em\u003e, resolvable OAS operation, as defined with a unique \u003ccode\u003eoperationId\u003c/code\u003e.  This field is mutually exclusive of the \u003ccode\u003eoperationRef\u003c/code\u003e field.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-linkparameters\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eparameters\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eMap[\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e, Any | \u003ca href=\"#runtime-expressions\"\u003e{expression}\u003c/a\u003e]\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA map representing parameters to pass to an operation as specified with \u003ccode\u003eoperationId\u003c/code\u003e or identified via \u003ccode\u003eoperationRef\u003c/code\u003e. The key is the parameter name to be used, whereas the value can be a constant or an expression to be evaluated and passed to the linked operation.  The parameter name can be qualified using the \u003ca href=\"#parameterIn\"\u003eparameter location\u003c/a\u003e \u003ccode\u003e[{in}.]{name}\u003c/code\u003e for operations that use the same parameter name in different locations (e.g. path.id).\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-linkrequestbody\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003erequestBody\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eAny | \u003ca href=\"#runtime-expressions\"\u003e{expression}\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA literal value or \u003ca href=\"#runtime-expressions\"\u003e{expression}\u003c/a\u003e to use as a request body when calling the target operation.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-linkdescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA description of the link. \u003ca href=\"http://spec.commonmark.org/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eCommonMark syntax\u003c/a\u003e MAY be used for rich text representation.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-linkserver\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eserver\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#server-object\"\u003eServer Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA server object to be used by the target operation.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis object MAY be extended with \u003ca href=\"#specification-extensions\"\u003eSpecification Extensions\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eA linked operation MUST be identified using either an \u003ccode\u003eoperationRef\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003eoperationId\u003c/code\u003e.\nIn the case of an \u003ccode\u003eoperationId\u003c/code\u003e, it MUST be unique and resolved in the scope of the OAS document.\nBecause of the potential for name clashes, the \u003ccode\u003eoperationRef\u003c/code\u003e syntax is preferred\nfor specifications with external references.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eExamples\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-examples\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Examples\" href=\"#examples\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eComputing a link from a request operation where the \u003ccode\u003e$request.path.id\u003c/code\u003e is used to pass a request parameter to the linked operation.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-yaml notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"paths:\n  /users/{id}:\n    parameters:\n    - name: id\n      in: path\n      required: true\n      description: the user identifier, as userId\n      schema:\n        type: string\n    get:\n      responses:\n        '200':\n          description: the user being returned\n          content:\n            application/json:\n              schema:\n                type: object\n                properties:\n                  uuid: # the unique user id\n                    type: string\n                    format: uuid\n          links:\n            address:\n              # the target link operationId\n              operationId: getUserAddress\n              parameters:\n                # get the `id` field from the request path parameter named `id`\n                userId: $request.path.id\n  # the path item of the linked operation\n  /users/{userid}/address:\n    parameters:\n    - name: userid\n      in: path\n      required: true\n      description: the user identifier, as userId\n      schema:\n        type: string\n    # linked operation\n    get:\n      operationId: getUserAddress\n      responses:\n        '200':\n          description: the user's address\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003epaths\u003c/span\u003e:\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e/users/{id}\u003c/span\u003e:\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eparameters\u003c/span\u003e:\n    - \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003ename\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003eid\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003ein\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003epath\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003erequired\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003etrue\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003edescription\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003ethe user identifier, as userId\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eschema\u003c/span\u003e:\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003etype\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003estring\u003c/span\u003e\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eget\u003c/span\u003e:\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eresponses\u003c/span\u003e:\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e200\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e:\n          \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003edescription\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003ethe user being returned\u003c/span\u003e\n          \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003econtent\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eapplication/json\u003c/span\u003e:\n              \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eschema\u003c/span\u003e:\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003etype\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003eobject\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eproperties\u003c/span\u003e:\n                  \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003euuid\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e the unique user id\u003c/span\u003e\n                    \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003etype\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003estring\u003c/span\u003e\n                    \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eformat\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003euuid\u003c/span\u003e\n          \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003elinks\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eaddress\u003c/span\u003e:\n              \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e the target link operationId\u003c/span\u003e\n              \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eoperationId\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003egetUserAddress\u003c/span\u003e\n              \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eparameters\u003c/span\u003e:\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e get the `id` field from the request path parameter named `id`\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003euserId\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e$request.path.id\u003c/span\u003e\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e the path item of the linked operation\u003c/span\u003e\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e/users/{userid}/address\u003c/span\u003e:\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eparameters\u003c/span\u003e:\n    - \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003ename\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003euserid\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003ein\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003epath\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003erequired\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003etrue\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003edescription\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003ethe user identifier, as userId\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eschema\u003c/span\u003e:\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003etype\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003estring\u003c/span\u003e\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e linked operation\u003c/span\u003e\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eget\u003c/span\u003e:\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eoperationId\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003egetUserAddress\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eresponses\u003c/span\u003e:\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e200\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e:\n          \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003edescription\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003ethe user's address\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhen a runtime expression fails to evaluate, no parameter value is passed to the target operation.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eValues from the response body can be used to drive a linked operation.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-yaml notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"links:\n  address:\n    operationId: getUserAddressByUUID\n    parameters:\n      # get the `uuid` field from the `uuid` field in the response body\n      userUuid: $response.body#/uuid\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003elinks\u003c/span\u003e:\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eaddress\u003c/span\u003e:\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eoperationId\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003egetUserAddressByUUID\u003c/span\u003e\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eparameters\u003c/span\u003e:\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e get the `uuid` field from the `uuid` field in the response body\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003euserUuid\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e$response.body#/uuid\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eClients follow all links at their discretion.\nNeither permissions, nor the capability to make a successful call to that link, is guaranteed\nsolely by the existence of a relationship.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eOperationRef Examples\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-operationref-examples\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: OperationRef Examples\" href=\"#operationref-examples\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAs references to \u003ccode\u003eoperationId\u003c/code\u003e MAY NOT be possible (the \u003ccode\u003eoperationId\u003c/code\u003e is an optional\nvalue), references MAY also be made through a relative \u003ccode\u003eoperationRef\u003c/code\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-yaml notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"links:\n  UserRepositories:\n    # returns array of '#/components/schemas/repository'\n    operationRef: '#/paths/~12.0~1repositories~1{username}/get'\n    parameters:\n      username: $response.body#/username\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003elinks\u003c/span\u003e:\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eUserRepositories\u003c/span\u003e:\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e returns array of '#/components/schemas/repository'\u003c/span\u003e\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eoperationRef\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e#/paths/~12.0~1repositories~1{username}/get\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eparameters\u003c/span\u003e:\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eusername\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e$response.body#/username\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eor an absolute \u003ccode\u003eoperationRef\u003c/code\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-yaml notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"links:\n  UserRepositories:\n    # returns array of '#/components/schemas/repository'\n    operationRef: 'https://na2.gigantic-server.com/#/paths/~12.0~1repositories~1{username}/get'\n    parameters:\n      username: $response.body#/username\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003elinks\u003c/span\u003e:\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eUserRepositories\u003c/span\u003e:\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e returns array of '#/components/schemas/repository'\u003c/span\u003e\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eoperationRef\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003ehttps://na2.gigantic-server.com/#/paths/~12.0~1repositories~1{username}/get\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eparameters\u003c/span\u003e:\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eusername\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e$response.body#/username\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eNote that in the use of \u003ccode\u003eoperationRef\u003c/code\u003e, the \u003cem\u003eescaped forward-slash\u003c/em\u003e is necessary when\nusing JSON references.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eRuntime Expressions\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-runtime-expressions\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Runtime Expressions\" href=\"#runtime-expressions\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eRuntime expressions allow defining values based on information that will only be available within the HTTP message in an actual API call.\nThis mechanism is used by \u003ca href=\"#link-object\"\u003eLink Objects\u003c/a\u003e and \u003ca href=\"#callback-object\"\u003eCallback Objects\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe runtime expression is defined by the following \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5234\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eABNF\u003c/a\u003e syntax\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-text-roff notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"      expression = ( \u0026quot;$url\u0026quot; | \u0026quot;$method\u0026quot; | \u0026quot;$statusCode\u0026quot; | \u0026quot;$request.\u0026quot; source | \u0026quot;$response.\u0026quot; source )\n      source = ( header-reference | query-reference | path-reference | body-reference ) \n      header-reference = \u0026quot;header.\u0026quot; token\n      query-reference = \u0026quot;query.\u0026quot; name \n      path-reference = \u0026quot;path.\u0026quot; name\n      body-reference = \u0026quot;body\u0026quot; [\u0026quot;#\u0026quot; fragment]\n      fragment = a JSON Pointer [RFC 6901](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6901) \n      name = *( char )\n      char = as per RFC [7159](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7159#section-7)\n      token = as per RFC [7230](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7230#section-3.2.6)\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e      expression = ( \"$url\" | \"$method\" | \"$statusCode\" | \"$request.\" source | \"$response.\" source )\n      source = ( header-reference | query-reference | path-reference | body-reference ) \n      header-reference = \"header.\" token\n      query-reference = \"query.\" name \n      path-reference = \"path.\" name\n      body-reference = \"body\" [\"#\" fragment]\n      fragment = a JSON Pointer [RFC 6901](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6901) \n      name = *( char )\n      char = as per RFC [7159](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7159#section-7)\n      token = as per RFC [7230](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7230#section-3.2.6)\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe \u003ccode\u003ename\u003c/code\u003e identifier is case-sensitive, whereas \u003ccode\u003etoken\u003c/code\u003e is not.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe table below provides examples of runtime expressions and examples of their use in a value:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eExamples\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-examples-1\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Examples\" href=\"#examples-1\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 Location\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"left\"\u003eexample expression\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"left\"\u003enotes\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eHTTP Method\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$method\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003eThe allowable values for the \u003ccode\u003e$method\u003c/code\u003e will be those for the HTTP operation.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eRequested media type\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$request.header.accept\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eRequest parameter\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$request.path.id\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003eRequest parameters MUST be declared in the \u003ccode\u003eparameters\u003c/code\u003e section of the parent operation or they cannot be evaluated. This includes request headers.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eRequest body property\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$request.body#/user/uuid\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003eIn operations which accept payloads, references may be made to portions of the \u003ccode\u003erequestBody\u003c/code\u003e or the entire body.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eRequest URL\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$url\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eResponse value\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$response.body#/status\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003eIn operations which return payloads, references may be made to portions of the response body or the entire body.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eResponse header\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$response.header.Server\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"left\"\u003eSingle header values only are available\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eRuntime expressions preserve the type of the referenced value.\nExpressions can be embedded into string values by surrounding the expression with \u003ccode\u003e{}\u003c/code\u003e curly braces.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eHeader Object\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-header-object\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Header Object\" href=\"#header-object\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe Header Object follows the structure of the \u003ca href=\"#parameter-object\"\u003eParameter Object\u003c/a\u003e with the following changes:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ename\u003c/code\u003e MUST NOT be specified, it is given in the corresponding \u003ccode\u003eheaders\u003c/code\u003e map.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ein\u003c/code\u003e MUST NOT be specified, it is implicitly in \u003ccode\u003eheader\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAll traits that are affected by the location MUST be applicable to a location of \u003ccode\u003eheader\u003c/code\u003e (for example, \u003ca href=\"#parameterStyle\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estyle\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eHeader Object Example\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-header-object-example\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Header Object align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eREQUIRED\u003c/strong\u003e. The name of the tag.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-tagdescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eCommonMark syntax\u003c/a\u003e MAY be used for rich text representation.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca referencing other components in the specification, internally and externally.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe Reference Object is defined by \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-pbryan-zyp-json-ref-03\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eJSON Reference\u003c/a\u003e and follows the same structure, behavior and rules.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFor this specification, reference resolution is accomplished as defined by the JSON Reference specification and not by the JSON Schema specification.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv dir=\"auto\"\u003eSchema Object\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-schema-object\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Schema Object\" href=\"#schema-object\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 Object allows the definition of input and output data types.\nThese types can be objects, but also primitives and arrays.\nThis object is an extended subset of the \u003ca href=\"http://json-schema.org/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eJSON Schema Specification Wright Draft 00\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFor more information about the properties, see \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-wright-json-schema-00\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eJSON Schema Core\u003c/a\u003e and \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-wright-json-schema-validation-00\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eJSON Schema Validation\u003c/a\u003e.\nUnless stated otherwise, the property definitions follow the JSON Schema.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eProperties\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-properties\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Properties\" href=\"#properties\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe following properties are taken directly from the JSON Schema definition and follow the same specifications:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003etitle\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003emultipleOf\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003emaximum\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eexclusiveMaximum\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eminimum\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eexclusiveMinimum\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003emaxLength\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eminLength\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003epattern (This string SHOULD be a valid regular expression, according to the \u003ca href=\"https://www.ecma-international.org/ecma-262/5.1/#sec-7.8.5\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eECMA 262 regular expression\u003c/a\u003e dialect)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003emaxItems\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eminItems\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003euniqueItems\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003emaxProperties\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eminProperties\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003erequired\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eenum\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe following properties are taken from the JSON Schema definition but their definitions were adjusted to the OpenAPI Specification.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003etype - Value MUST be a string. Multiple types via an array are not supported.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eallOf - Inline or referenced schema MUST be of a \u003ca href=\"#schema-object\"\u003eSchema Object\u003c/a\u003e and not a standard JSON Schema.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eoneOf - Inline or referenced schema MUST be of a \u003ca href=\"#schema-object\"\u003eSchema Object\u003c/a\u003e and not a standard JSON Schema.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eanyOf - Inline or referenced schema MUST be of a \u003ca href=\"#schema-object\"\u003eSchema Object\u003c/a\u003e and not a standard JSON Schema.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003enot - Inline or referenced schema MUST be of a \u003ca href=\"#schema-object\"\u003eSchema Object\u003c/a\u003e and not a standard JSON Schema.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eitems - Value MUST be an object and not an array. Inline or referenced schema MUST be of a \u003ca href=\"#schema-object\"\u003eSchema Object\u003c/a\u003e and not a standard JSON Schema. \u003ccode\u003eitems\u003c/code\u003e MUST be present if the \u003ccode\u003etype\u003c/code\u003e is \u003ccode\u003earray\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eproperties - Property definitions MUST be a \u003ca href=\"#schema-object\"\u003eSchema Object\u003c/a\u003e and not a standard JSON Schema (inline or referenced).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eadditionalProperties - Value can be boolean or object. Inline or referenced schema MUST be of a \u003ca href=\"#schema-object\"\u003eSchema Object\u003c/a\u003e and not a standard JSON Schema.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003edescription - \u003ca href=\"http://spec.commonmark.org/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eCommonMark syntax\u003c/a\u003e MAY be used for rich text representation.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eformat - See \u003ca href=\"#dataTypeFormat\"\u003eData Type Formats\u003c/a\u003e for further details. While relying on JSON Schema's defined formats, the OAS offers a few additional predefined formats.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003edefault - The default value represents what would be assumed by the consumer of the input as the value of the schema if one is not provided. Unlike JSON Schema, the value MUST conform to the defined type for the Schema Object defined at the same level. For example, if \u003ccode\u003etype\u003c/code\u003e is \u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e, then \u003ccode\u003edefault\u003c/code\u003e can be \u003ccode\u003e\"foo\"\u003c/code\u003e but cannot be \u003ccode\u003e1\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAlternatively, any time a Schema Object can be used, a \u003ca href=\"#reference-object\"\u003eReference Object\u003c/a\u003e can be used in its place. This allows referencing definitions instead of defining them inline.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAdditional properties defined by the JSON Schema specification that are not mentioned here are strictly unsupported.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOther than the JSON Schema subset fields, the following fields MAY be used for further schema documentation:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eFixed Fields\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-fixed-fields-20\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Fixed Fields\" href=\"#fixed-fields-20\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eField Name\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-schemanullable\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003enullable\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eboolean\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAllows sending a \u003ccode\u003enull\u003c/code\u003e value for the defined schema. Default value is \u003ccode\u003efalse\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-schemadiscriminator\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ediscriminator\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#discriminator-object\"\u003eDiscriminator Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAdds support for polymorphism. The discriminator is an object name that is used to differentiate between other schemas which may satisfy the payload description. See \u003ca href=\"#composition-and-inheritance-polymorphism\"\u003eComposition and Inheritance\u003c/a\u003e for more details.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-schemareadonly\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ereadOnly\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eboolean\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eRelevant only for Schema \u003ccode\u003e\"properties\"\u003c/code\u003e definitions. Declares the property as \"read only\". This means that it MAY be sent as part of a response but SHOULD NOT be sent as part of the request. If the property is marked as \u003ccode\u003ereadOnly\u003c/code\u003e being \u003ccode\u003etrue\u003c/code\u003e and is in the \u003ccode\u003erequired\u003c/code\u003e list, the \u003ccode\u003erequired\u003c/code\u003e will take effect on the response only. A property MUST NOT be marked as both \u003ccode\u003ereadOnly\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003ewriteOnly\u003c/code\u003e being \u003ccode\u003etrue\u003c/code\u003e. Default value is \u003ccode\u003efalse\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-schemawriteonly\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ewriteOnly\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eboolean\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eRelevant only for Schema \u003ccode\u003e\"properties\"\u003c/code\u003e definitions. Declares the property as \"write only\". Therefore, it MAY be sent as part of a request but SHOULD NOT be sent as part of the response. If the property is marked as \u003ccode\u003ewriteOnly\u003c/code\u003e being \u003ccode\u003etrue\u003c/code\u003e and is in the \u003ccode\u003erequired\u003c/code\u003e list, the \u003ccode\u003erequired\u003c/code\u003e will take effect on the request only. A property MUST NOT be marked as both \u003ccode\u003ereadOnly\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003ewriteOnly\u003c/code\u003e being \u003ccode\u003etrue\u003c/code\u003e. Default value is \u003ccode\u003efalse\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-schemaxml\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003exml\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#xml-object\"\u003eXML Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThis MAY be used only on properties schemas. It has no effect on root schemas. Adds additional metadata to describe the XML representation of this property.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-schemaexternaldocs\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eexternalDocs\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#external-documentation-object\"\u003eExternal Documentation Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAdditional external documentation for this schema.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-schemaexample\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eexample\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA free-form property to include an example of an instance for this schema. To represent examples that cannot be naturally represented in JSON or YAML, a string value can be used to contain the example with escaping where necessary.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-schemadeprecated\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e deprecated\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eboolean\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eSpecifies that a schema is deprecated and SHOULD be transitioned out of usage. Default value is \u003ccode\u003efalse\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis object MAY be extended with \u003ca href=\"#specification-extensions\"\u003eSpecification Extensions\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch6 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eComposition and Inheritance (Polymorphism)\u003c/h6\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-composition-and-inheritance-polymorphism\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Composition and Inheritance (Polymorphism)\" href=\"#composition-and-inheritance-polymorphism\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe OpenAPI Specification allows combining and extending model definitions using the \u003ccode\u003eallOf\u003c/code\u003e property of JSON Schema, in effect offering model composition.\n\u003ccode\u003eallOf\u003c/code\u003e takes an array of object definitions that are validated \u003cem\u003eindependently\u003c/em\u003e but together compose a single object.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhile composition offers model extensibility, it does not imply a hierarchy between the models.\nTo support polymorphism, the OpenAPI Specification adds the \u003ccode\u003ediscriminator\u003c/code\u003e field.\nWhen used, the \u003ccode\u003ediscriminator\u003c/code\u003e will be the name of the property that decides which schema definition validates the structure of the model.\nAs such, the \u003ccode\u003ediscriminator\u003c/code\u003e field MUST be a required field.\nThere are two ways to define the value of a discriminator for an inheriting instance.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eUse the schema name.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eOverride the schema name by overriding the property with a new value. If a new value exists, this takes precedence over the schema name.\nAs such, inline schema definitions, which do not have a given id, \u003cem\u003ecannot\u003c/em\u003e be used in polymorphism.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch6 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eXML Modeling\u003c/h6\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-xml-modeling\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: XML Modeling\" href=\"#xml-modeling\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 property allows extra definitions when translating the JSON definition to XML.\nThe \u003ca href=\"#xml-object\"\u003eXML Object\u003c/a\u003e contains additional information about the available
