2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhen request bodies or response payloads may be one of a number of different schemas, a \u003ccode\u003ediscriminator\u003c/code\u003e object can be used to aid in serialization, deserialization, and validation.  The discriminator is a specific object in a schema which is used to inform the consumer of the specification of an alternative schema based on the value associated with it.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhen using the discriminator, \u003cem\u003einline\u003c/em\u003e schemas will not be considered.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eFixed Fields\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-fixed-fields-21\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Fixed Fields\" href=\"#fixed-fields-21\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eField Name\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-propertyname\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003epropertyName\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eREQUIRED\u003c/strong\u003e. The name of the property in the payload that will hold the discriminator value.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-discriminatormapping\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e mapping\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eMap[\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e]\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAn object to hold mappings between payload values and schema names or references.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe discriminator attribute is legal only when using one of the composite keywords \u003ccode\u003eoneOf\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eanyOf\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eallOf\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIn OAS 3.0, a response payload MAY be described to be exactly one of any number of types:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-text-roff notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"MyResponseType:\n  oneOf:\n  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Cat'\n  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Dog'\n  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Lizard'\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003eMyResponseType:\n  oneOf:\n  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Cat'\n  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Dog'\n  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Lizard'\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ewhich means the payload \u003cem\u003eMUST\u003c/em\u003e, by validation, match exactly one of the schemas described by \u003ccode\u003eCat\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eDog\u003c/code\u003e, or \u003ccode\u003eLizard\u003c/code\u003e.  In this case, a discriminator MAY act as a \"hint\" to shortcut validation and selection of the matching schema which may be a costly operation, depending on the complexity of the schema. We can then describe exactly which field tells us which schema to use:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-text-roff notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"MyResponseType:\n  oneOf:\n  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Cat'\n  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Dog'\n  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Lizard'\n  discriminator:\n    propertyName: pet_type\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003eMyResponseType:\n  oneOf:\n  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Cat'\n  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Dog'\n  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Lizard'\n  discriminator:\n    propertyName: pet_type\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe expectation now is that a property with name \u003ccode\u003epet_type\u003c/code\u003e \u003cem\u003eMUST\u003c/em\u003e be present in the response payload, and the value will correspond to the name of a schema defined in the OAS document.  Thus the response payload:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-text-roff notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"{\n  \u0026quot;id\u0026quot;: 12345,\n  \u0026quot;pet_type\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;Cat\u0026quot;\n}\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e{\n  \"id\": 12345,\n  \"pet_type\": \"Cat\"\n}\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWill indicate that the \u003ccode\u003eCat\u003c/code\u003e schema be used in conjunction with this payload.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIn scenarios where the value of the discriminator field does not match the schema name or implicit mapping is not possible, an optional \u003ccode\u003emapping\u003c/code\u003e definition MAY be used:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-text-roff notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"MyResponseType:\n  oneOf:\n  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Cat'\n  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Dog'\n  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Lizard'\n  - $ref: 'https://gigantic-server.com/schemas/Monster/schema.json'\n  discriminator:\n    propertyName: pet_type\n    mapping:\n      dog: '#/components/schemas/Dog'\n      monster: 'https://gigantic-server.com/schemas/Monster/schema.json'\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003eMyResponseType:\n  oneOf:\n  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Cat'\n  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Dog'\n  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Lizard'\n  - $ref: 'https://gigantic-server.com/schemas/Monster/schema.json'\n  discriminator:\n    propertyName: pet_type\n    mapping:\n      dog: '#/components/schemas/Dog'\n      monster: 'https://gigantic-server.com/schemas/Monster/schema.json'\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eHere the discriminator \u003cem\u003evalue\u003c/em\u003e of \u003ccode\u003edog\u003c/code\u003e will map to the schema \u003ccode\u003e#/components/schemas/Dog\u003c/code\u003e, rather than the default (implicit) value of \u003ccode\u003eDog\u003c/code\u003e.  If the discriminator \u003cem\u003evalue\u003c/em\u003e does not match an implicit or explicit mapping, no schema can be determined and validation SHOULD fail. Mapping keys MUST be string values, but tooling MAY convert response values to strings for comparison.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhen used in conjunction with the \u003ccode\u003eanyOf\u003c/code\u003e construct, the use of the discriminator can avoid ambiguity where multiple schemas may satisfy a single payload.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIn both the \u003ccode\u003eoneOf\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003eanyOf\u003c/code\u003e use cases, all possible schemas MUST be listed explicitly.  To avoid redundancy, the discriminator MAY be added to a parent schema definition, and all schemas comprising the parent schema in an \u003ccode\u003eallOf\u003c/code\u003e construct may be used as an alternate schema.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFor example:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-text-roff notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"components:\n  schemas:\n    Pet:\n      type: object\n      required:\n      - pet_type\n      properties:\n        pet_type:\n          type: string\n      discriminator:\n        propertyName: pet_type\n        mapping:\n          cachorro: Dog\n    Cat:\n      allOf:\n      - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Pet'\n      - type: object\n        # all other properties specific to a `Cat`\n        properties:\n          name:\n            type: string\n    Dog:\n      allOf:\n      - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Pet'\n      - type: object\n        # all other properties specific to a `Dog`\n        properties:\n          bark:\n            type: string\n    Lizard:\n      allOf:\n      - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Pet'\n      - type: object\n        # all other properties specific to a `Lizard`\n        properties:\n          lovesRocks:\n            type: boolean\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003ecomponents:\n  schemas:\n    Pet:\n      type: object\n      required:\n      - pet_type\n      properties:\n        pet_type:\n          type: string\n      discriminator:\n        propertyName: pet_type\n        mapping:\n          cachorro: Dog\n    Cat:\n      allOf:\n      - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Pet'\n      - type: object\n        # all other properties specific to a `Cat`\n        properties:\n          name:\n            type: string\n    Dog:\n      allOf:\n      - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Pet'\n      - type: object\n        # all other properties specific to a `Dog`\n        properties:\n          bark:\n            type: string\n    Lizard:\n      allOf:\n      - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Pet'\n      - type: object\n        # all other properties specific to a `Lizard`\n        properties:\n          lovesRocks:\n            type: boolean\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ea payload like this:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-text-roff notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"{\n  \u0026quot;pet_type\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;Cat\u0026quot;,\n  \u0026quot;name\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;misty\u0026quot;\n}\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e{\n  \"pet_type\": \"Cat\",\n  \"name\": \"misty\"\n}\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ewill indicate that the \u003ccode\u003eCat\u003c/code\u003e schema be used.  Likewise this schema:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-text-roff notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"{\n  \u0026quot;pet_type\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;cachorro\u0026quot;,\n  \u0026quot;bark\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;soft\u0026quot;\n}\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e{\n  \"pet_type\": \"cachorro\",\n  \"bark\": \"soft\"\n}\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ewill map to \u003ccode\u003eDog\u003c/code\u003e because of the definition in the \u003ccode\u003emappings\u003c/code\u003e element.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eXML Object\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-xml-object\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: XML Object\" href=\"#xml-object\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eA metadata object that allows for more fine-tuned XML model definitions.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhen using arrays, XML element names are \u003cem\u003enot\u003c/em\u003e inferred (for singular/plural forms) and the \u003ccode\u003ename\u003c/code\u003e property SHOULD be used to add that information.\nSee examples for expected behavior.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eFixed Fields\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-fixed-fields-22\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Fixed Fields\" href=\"#fixed-fields-22\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eField Name\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-xmlname\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ename\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eReplaces the name of the element/attribute used for the described schema property. When defined within \u003ccode\u003eitems\u003c/code\u003e, it will affect the name of the individual XML elements within the list. When defined alongside \u003ccode\u003etype\u003c/code\u003e being \u003ccode\u003earray\u003c/code\u003e (outside the \u003ccode\u003eitems\u003c/code\u003e), it will affect the wrapping element and only if \u003ccode\u003ewrapped\u003c/code\u003e is \u003ccode\u003etrue\u003c/code\u003e. If \u003ccode\u003ewrapped\u003c/code\u003e is \u003ccode\u003efalse\u003c/code\u003e, it will be ignored.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-xmlnamespace\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003enamespace\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe URI of the namespace definition. Value MUST be in the form of an absolute URI.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-xmlprefix\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eprefix\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe prefix to be used for the \u003ca href=\"#xmlName\"\u003ename\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-xmlattribute\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eattribute\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eboolean\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eDeclares whether the property definition translates to an attribute instead of an element. Default value is \u003ccode\u003efalse\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-xmlwrapped\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ewrapped\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eboolean\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eMAY be used only for an array definition. Signifies whether the array is wrapped (for example, \u003ccode\u003e\u0026lt;books\u0026gt;\u0026lt;book/\u0026gt;\u0026lt;book/\u0026gt;\u0026lt;/books\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e) or unwrapped (\u003ccode\u003e\u0026lt;book/\u0026gt;\u0026lt;book/\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e). Default value is \u003ccode\u003efalse\u003c/code\u003e. The definition takes effect only when defined alongside \u003ccode\u003etype\u003c/code\u003e being \u003ccode\u003earray\u003c/code\u003e (outside the \u003ccode\u003eitems\u003c/code\u003e).\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis object MAY be extended with \u003ca href=\"#specification-extensions\"\u003eSpecification Extensions\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eXML Object Examples\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-xml-object-examples\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: XML Object Examples\" href=\"#xml-object-examples\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe examples of the XML object definitions are included inside a property definition of a \u003ca href=\"#schema-object\"\u003eSchema Object\u003c/a\u003e with a sample of the XML representation of it.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch6 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eNo XML Element\u003c/h6\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-no-xml-element\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: No XML Element\" href=\"#no-xml-element\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 when the array is wrapped, if a name is not explicitly defined, the same name will be used both internally and externally:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-json notranslate dir=\"auto\"\u003eSecurity Scheme Object\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-security-scheme-object\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Security Scheme Object\" 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eDefines a security scheme that can be used by the operations.\nSupported schemes are HTTP authentication, an API key (either as a header or as a query parameter), OAuth2's common flows (implicit, password, application and access code) as defined in \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eRFC6749\u003c/a\u003e, and \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-ietf-oauth-discovery-06\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eOpenID Connect Discovery\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eFixed Fields\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-fixed-fields-23\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Fixed Fields\" href=\"#fixed-fields-23\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eField Name\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eApplies To\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-securityschemetype\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etype\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eREQUIRED\u003c/strong\u003e. The type of the security scheme. Valid values are \u003ccode\u003e\"apiKey\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"http\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"oauth2\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"openIdConnect\"\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-securityschemedescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA short description for security scheme. \u003ca href=\"http://spec.commonmark.org/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eCommonMark syntax\u003c/a\u003e MAY be used for rich text representation.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-securityschemename\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ename\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eapiKey\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eREQUIRED\u003c/strong\u003e. The name of the header, query or cookie parameter to be used.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-securityschemein\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ein\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eapiKey\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eREQUIRED\u003c/strong\u003e. The location of the API key. Valid values are \u003ccode\u003e\"query\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"header\"\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003e\"cookie\"\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-securityschemescheme\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003escheme\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003ehttp\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eREQUIRED\u003c/strong\u003e. The name of the HTTP Authorization scheme to be used in the \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-5.1\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eAuthorization header as defined in RFC7235\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-securityschemebearerformat\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ebearerFormat\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003ehttp\u003c/code\u003e (\u003ccode\u003e\"bearer\"\u003c/code\u003e)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA hint to the client to identify how the bearer token is formatted.  Bearer tokens are usually generated by an authorization server, so this information is primarily for documentation purposes.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-securityschemeflows\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eflows\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#oauth-flows-object\"\u003eOAuth Flows Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eoauth2\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eREQUIRED\u003c/strong\u003e. An object containing configuration information for the flow types supported.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-securityschemeopenidconnecturl\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eopenIdConnectUrl\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eopenIdConnect\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eREQUIRED\u003c/strong\u003e. OpenId Connect URL to discover OAuth2 configuration values. This MUST be in the form of a URL.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis object MAY be extended with \u003ca href=\"#specification-extensions\"\u003eSpecification Extensions\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003ehttps://example.com/api/oauth/dialog\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e,\n      \u003cspan To\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-oauthflowauthorizationurl\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eauthorizationUrl\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eoauth2\u003c/code\u003e (\u003ccode\u003e\"implicit\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"authorizationCode\"\u003c/code\u003e)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eREQUIRED\u003c/strong\u003e. The authorization URL to be used for this flow. This MUST be in the form of a URL.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-oauthflowtokenurl\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etokenUrl\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eoauth2\u003c/code\u003e (\u003ccode\u003e\"password\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"clientCredentials\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"authorizationCode\"\u003c/code\u003e)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eREQUIRED\u003c/strong\u003e. The token URL to be used for this flow. This MUST be in the form of a URL.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-oauthflowrefreshurl\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003erefreshUrl\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eoauth2\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe URL to be used for obtaining refresh tokens. This MUST be in the form of a URL.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-oauthflowscopes\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003escopes\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eMap[\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e]\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eoauth2\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eREQUIRED\u003c/strong\u003e. The available scopes for the OAuth2 security scheme. A map between the scope name and a short description for it.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"{\n  \u0026quot;type\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;oauth2\u0026quot;,\n  \u0026quot;flows\u0026quot;: {\n    \u0026quot;implicit\u0026quot;: {\n      \u0026quot;authorizationUrl\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;https://example.com/api/oauth/dialog\u0026quot;,\n      \u0026quot;scopes\u0026quot;: {\n        \u0026quot;write:pets\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;modify pets in your account\u0026quot;,\n        \u0026quot;read:pets\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;read your pets\u0026quot;\n      }\n    },\n    \u0026quot;authorizationCode\u0026quot;: {\n      \u0026quot;authorizationUrl\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;https://example.com/api/oauth/dialog\u0026quot;,\n      \u0026quot;tokenUrl\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;https://example.com/api/oauth/token\u0026quot;,\n      \u0026quot;scopes\u0026quot;: {\n        \u0026quot;write:pets\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;modify pets in your account\u0026quot;,\n        \u0026quot;read:pets\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;read your pets\u0026quot;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e{\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e\"type\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eSecurity Requirement Object\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-security-requirement-object\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Security Requirement Object\" href=\"#security-requirement-object\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eLists the required security schemes to execute this operation.\nThe name used for each property MUST correspond to a security scheme declared in the \u003ca href=\"#componentsSecuritySchemes\"\u003eSecurity Schemes\u003c/a\u003e under the \u003ca href=\"#components-object\"\u003eComponents Object\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSecurity Requirement Objects that contain multiple schemes require that all schemes MUST be satisfied for a request to be authorized.\nThis enables support for scenarios where multiple query parameters or HTTP headers are required to convey security information.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhen a list of Security Requirement Objects is defined on the \u003ca href=\"#openapi-object\"\u003eOpen API object\u003c/a\u003e or \u003ca href=\"#operation-object\"\u003eOperation Object\u003c/a\u003e, only one of Security Requirement Objects in the list needs to be satisfied to authorize the request.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ePatterned Fields\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-patterned-fields-3\" class=\"anchor\" name=\"user-content-securityrequirementsname\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e{name}\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e[\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e]\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eEach name MUST correspond to a security scheme which is declared in the \u003ca href=\"#componentsSecuritySchemes\"\u003eSecurity Schemes\u003c/a\u003e under the \u003ca href=\"#components-object\"\u003eComponents Object\u003c/a\u003e. If the security scheme is of type \u003ccode\u003e\"oauth2\"\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003e\"openIdConnect\"\u003c/code\u003e, then the value is a list of scope names required for the execution. For other security scheme types, the array MUST be empty.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv dir=\"auto\"\u003eSpecification Extensions\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-specification-extensions\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Specification Extensions\" href=\"#specification-extensions\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhile the OpenAPI Specification tries to accommodate most use cases, additional data can be added to extend the specification at certain points.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe extensions properties are implemented as patterned fields that are always prefixed by \u003ccode\u003e\"x-\"\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eField Pattern\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-infoextensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x-\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAllows extensions to the OpenAPI Schema. The field name MUST begin with \u003ccode\u003ex-\u003c/code\u003e, for example, \u003ccode\u003ex-internal-id\u003c/code\u003e. The value can be \u003ccode\u003enull\u003c/code\u003e, a primitive, an array or an object. Can have any valid JSON format value.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe extensions may or may not be supported by the available tooling, but those may be extended as well to add requested support (if tools are internal or open-sourced).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eSecurity Filtering\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-security-filtering\" class=\"anchor\" 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSome objects in the OpenAPI Specification MAY be declared and remain empty, or be completely removed, even though they are inherently the core of the API documentation.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe reasoning is to allow an additional layer of access control over the documentation.\nWhile not part of the specification itself, certain libraries MAY choose to allow access to parts of the documentation based on some form of authentication/authorization.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTwo examples of this:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe \u003ca href=\"#paths-object\"\u003ePaths Object\u003c/a\u003e MAY be empty. It may be counterintuitive, but this may tell the viewer that they got to the right place, but can't access any documentation. They'd still have access to the \u003ca href=\"#info-object\"\u003eInfo Object\u003c/a\u003e which may contain additional information regarding authentication.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe \u003ca href=\"#path-item-object\"\u003ePath Item Object\u003c/a\u003e MAY be empty. In this case, the viewer will be aware that the path exists, but will not be able to see any of its operations or parameters. This is different than hiding the path itself from the \u003ca href=\"#paths-object\"\u003ePaths Object\u003c/a\u003e, so the user will not be aware of its existence. This allows the documentation provider to finely control what the viewer can see.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eAppendix A: Revision History\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-appendix-a-revision-history\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Appendix A: OpenAPI Specification","","#### Version 3.0.1","","The key words \"MUST\", \"MUST NOT\", \"REQUIRED\", \"SHALL\", \"SHALL NOT\", \"SHOULD\", \"SHOULD NOT\", \"RECOMMENDED\", \"NOT RECOMMENDED\", \"MAY\", and \"OPTIONAL\" in this document are to be interpreted as described in [BCP 14](https://tools.ietf.org/html/bcp14) [RFC2119](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc2119) [RFC8174](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8174) when, and only when, they appear in all capitals, as shown here.","","This document is licensed under [The Apache License, Version 2.0](https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html).","","## Introduction","","The OpenAPI Specification (OAS) defines a standard, language-agnostic interface to RESTful APIs which allows both humans and computers to discover and understand the capabilities of the service without access to source code, documentation, or through network traffic inspection. When properly defined, a consumer can understand and interact with the remote service with a minimal amount of implementation logic.","","An OpenAPI definition can then be used by documentation generation tools to display the API, code generation tools to generate servers and clients in various programming languages, testing tools, and many other use cases.","","## Table of Contents","\u003c!-- TOC depthFrom:1 depthTo:3 withLinks:1 updateOnSave:1 orderedList:0 --\u003e","","- [Definitions](#definitions)","\t- [OpenAPI Document](#openapi-document)","\t- [Path Templating](#path-templating)","\t- [Media Types](#media-types)","\t- [HTTP Status Codes](#http-status-codes)","- [Specification](#specification)","\t- [Versions](#versions)","\t- [Format](#format)","\t- [Document Structure](#document-structure)","\t- [Data Types](#data-types)","\t- [Rich Text Formatting](#rich-text-formatting)","\t- [Relative References In URLs](#relative-references-in-urls)","\t- [Schema](#schema)","\t\t- [OpenAPI Object](#openapi-object)","\t\t- [Info Object](#info-object)","\t\t- [Contact Object](#contact-object)","\t\t- [License Object](#license-object)","\t\t- [Server Object](#server-object)","\t\t- [Server Variable Object](#server-variable-object)","\t\t- [Components Object](#components-object)","\t\t- [Paths Object](#paths-object)","\t\t- [Path Item Object](#path-item-object)","\t\t- [Operation Object](#operation-object)","\t\t- [External Documentation Object](#external-documentation-object)","\t\t- [Parameter Object](#parameter-object)","\t\t- [Request Body Object](#request-body-object)","\t\t- [Media Type Object](#media-type-object)","\t\t- [Encoding Object](#encoding-object)","\t\t- [Responses Object](#responses-object)","\t\t- [Response Object](#response-object)","\t\t- [Callback Object](#callback-object)","\t\t- [Example Object](#example-object)","\t\t- [Link Object](#link-object)","\t\t- [Header Object](#header-object)","\t\t- [Tag Object](#tag-object)","\t\t- [Reference Object](#reference-object)","\t\t- [Schema Object](#schema-object)","\t\t- [Discriminator Object](#discriminator-object)","\t\t- [XML Object](#xml-object)","\t\t- [Security Scheme Object](#security-scheme-object)","\t\t- [OAuth Flows Object](#oauth-flows-object)","\t\t- [OAuth Flow Object](#oauth-flow-object)","\t\t- [Security Requirement Object](#security-requirement-object)","\t- [Specification Extensions](#specification-extensions)","\t- [Security Filtering](#security-filtering)","- [Appendix A: Revision History](#appendix-a-revision-history)","\t","","\u003c!-- /TOC --\u003e","","## Definitions","","##### OpenAPI Document","A document (or set of documents) that defines or describes an API. An OpenAPI definition uses and conforms to the OpenAPI Specification.","","##### Path Templating","Path templating refers to the usage of curly braces ({}) to mark a section of a URL path as replaceable using path parameters.","","##### Media Types","Media type definitions are spread across several resources.","The media type definitions SHOULD be in compliance with [RFC6838](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6838).","","Some examples of possible media type definitions:","```","  text/plain; charset=utf-8","  application/json","  application/vnd.github+json","  application/vnd.github.v3+json","  application/vnd.github.v3.raw+json","  application/vnd.github.v3.text+json","  application/vnd.github.v3.html+json","  application/vnd.github.v3.full+json","  application/vnd.github.v3.diff","  application/vnd.github.v3.patch","```","##### HTTP Status Codes","The HTTP Status Codes are used to indicate the status of the executed operation.","The available status codes are defined by [RFC7231](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6) and registered status codes are listed in the [IANA Status Code Registry](https://www.iana.org/assignments/http-status-codes/http-status-codes.xhtml).","","## Specification","","### Versions","","The OpenAPI Specification is versioned using [Semantic Versioning 2.0.0](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html) (semver) and follows the semver specification.","","The `major`.`minor` portion of the semver (for example `3.0`) SHALL designate the OAS feature set. Typically, *`.patch`* versions address errors in this document, not the feature set. Tooling which supports OAS 3.0 SHOULD be compatible with all OAS 3.0.\\* versions. The patch version SHOULD NOT be considered by tooling, making no distinction between `3.0.0` and `3.0.1` for example.","","Subsequent minor version releases of the OpenAPI Specification (incrementing the `minor` version number) SHOULD NOT interfere with tooling developed to a lower minor version and same major version.  Thus a hypothetical `3.1.0` specification SHOULD be usable with tooling designed for `3.0.0`.","","An OpenAPI document compatible with OAS 3.\\*.\\* contains a required [`openapi`](#oasVersion) field which designates the semantic version of the OAS that it uses. (OAS 2.0 documents contain a top-level version field named [`swagger`](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/master/versions/2.0.md#swaggerObject) and value `\"2.0\"`.)","","### Format","","An OpenAPI document that conforms to the OpenAPI Specification is itself a JSON object, which may be represented either in JSON or YAML format.","","For example, if a field has an array value, the JSON array representation will be used:","","```json","{","   \"field\": [ 1, 2, 3 ]","}","```","All field names in the specification are **case sensitive**.","","The schema exposes two types of fields: Fixed fields, which have a declared name, and Patterned fields, which declare a regex pattern for the field name.","","Patterned fields MUST have unique names within the containing object.","","In order to preserve the ability to round-trip between YAML and JSON formats, YAML version [1.2](http://www.yaml.org/spec/1.2/spec.html) is RECOMMENDED along with some additional constraints:","","- Tags MUST be limited to those allowed by the [JSON Schema ruleset](http://www.yaml.org/spec/1.2/spec.html#id2803231).","- Keys used in YAML maps MUST be limited to a scalar string, as defined by the [YAML Failsafe schema ruleset](http://yaml.org/spec/1.2/spec.html#id2802346).","","**Note:** While APIs may be defined by OpenAPI documents in either YAML or JSON format, the API request and response bodies and other content are not required to be JSON or YAML.","","### Document Structure","","An OpenAPI document MAY be made up of a single document or be divided into multiple, connected parts at the discretion of the user. In the latter case, `$ref` fields MUST be used in the specification to reference those parts as follows from the [JSON Schema](http://json-schema.org) definitions.","","It is RECOMMENDED that the root OpenAPI document be named: `openapi.json` or `openapi.yaml`.","","### Data Types","","Primitive data types in the OAS are based on the types supported by the [JSON Schema Specification Wright Draft 00](https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-wright-json-schema-00#section-4.2).","Note that `integer` as a type is also supported and is defined as a JSON number without a fraction or exponent part.","`null` is not supported as a type (see [`nullable`](#schemaNullable) for an alternative solution).","Models are defined using the [Schema Object](#schema-object), which is an extended subset of JSON Schema Specification Wright Draft 00.","","\u003ca name=\"dataTypeFormat\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ePrimitives have an optional modifier property: `format`.","OAS uses several known formats to define in fine detail the data type being used.","However, to support documentation needs, the `format` property is an open `string`-valued property, and can have any value.","Formats such as `\"email\"`, `\"uuid\"`, and so on, MAY be used even though undefined by this specification.","Types that are not accompanied by a `format` property follow the type definition in the JSON Schema. Tools that do not recognize a specific `format` MAY default back to the `type` alone, as if the `format` is not specified.","","The formats defined by the OAS are:","","Common Name | [`type`](#data-types) | [`format`](#dataTypeFormat) | Comments","----------- | ------ | -------- | --------","integer | `integer` | `int32` | signed 32 bits","long | `integer` | `int64` | signed 64 bits","float | `number` | `float` | |","double | `number` | `double` | |","string | `string` | | |","byte | `string` | `byte` | base64 encoded characters","binary | `string` | `binary` | any sequence of octets","boolean | `boolean` | | |","date | `string` | `date` | As defined by `full-date` - [RFC3339](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3339#section-5.6)","dateTime | `string` | `date-time` | As defined by `date-time` - [RFC3339](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3339#section-5.6)","password | `string` | `password` | A hint to UIs to obscure input.","","### Rich Text Formatting","Throughout the specification `description` fields are noted as supporting CommonMark markdown formatting.","Where OpenAPI tooling renders rich text it MUST support, at a minimum, markdown syntax as described by [CommonMark 0.27](http://spec.commonmark.org/0.27/). Tooling MAY choose to ignore some CommonMark features to address security concerns.","","### Relative References in URLs","","Unless specified otherwise, all properties that are URLs MAY be relative references as defined by [RFC3986](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3986#section-4.2).","Relative references are resolved using the URLs defined in the [`Server Object`](#server-object) as a Base URI.","","Relative references used in `$ref` are processed as per [JSON Reference](https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-pbryan-zyp-json-ref-03), using the URL of the current document as the base URI. See also the [Reference Object](#reference-object).","","### Schema","","In the following description, if a field is not explicitly **REQUIRED** or described with a MUST or SHALL, it can be considered OPTIONAL.","","#### OpenAPI Object","","This is the root document object of the [OpenAPI document](#openapi-document).","","##### Fixed Fields","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"oasVersion\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eopenapi | `string` | **REQUIRED**. This string MUST be the [semantic version number](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html) of the [OpenAPI Specification version](#versions) that the OpenAPI document uses. The `openapi` field SHOULD be used by tooling specifications and clients to interpret the OpenAPI document. This is *not* related to the API [`info.version`](#infoVersion) string.","\u003ca name=\"oasInfo\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003einfo | [Info Object](#info-object) | **REQUIRED**. Provides metadata about the API. The metadata MAY be used by tooling as required.","\u003ca name=\"oasServers\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eservers | [[Server Object](#server-object)] | An array of Server Objects, which provide connectivity information to a target server. If the `servers` property is not provided, or is an empty array, the default value would be a [Server Object](#server-object) with a [url](#serverUrl) value of `/`.","\u003ca name=\"oasPaths\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003epaths | [Paths Object](#paths-object) | **REQUIRED**. The available paths and operations for the API.","\u003ca name=\"oasComponents\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ecomponents | [Components Object](#components-object) | An element to hold various schemas for the specification.","\u003ca name=\"oasSecurity\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003esecurity | [[Security Requirement Object](#security-requirement-object)] | A declaration of which security mechanisms can be used across the API. The list of values includes alternative security requirement objects that can be used. Only one of the security requirement objects need to be satisfied to authorize a request. Individual operations can override this definition.","\u003ca name=\"oasTags\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etags | [[Tag Object](#tag-object)] | A list of tags used by the specification with additional metadata. The order of the tags can be used to reflect on their order by the parsing tools. Not all tags that are used by the [Operation Object](#operation-object) must be declared. The tags that are not declared MAY be organized randomly or based on the tools' logic. Each tag name in the list MUST be unique.","\u003ca name=\"oasExternalDocs\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eexternalDocs | [External Documentation Object](#external-documentation-object) | Additional external documentation.","","This object MAY be extended with [Specification Extensions](#specification-extensions).","","#### Info Object","","The object provides metadata about the API.","The metadata MAY be used by the clients if needed, and MAY be presented in editing or documentation generation tools for convenience.","","##### Fixed Fields","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"infoTitle\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etitle | `string` | **REQUIRED**. The title of the application.","\u003ca name=\"infoDescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription | `string` | A short description of the application. [CommonMark syntax](http://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.","\u003ca name=\"infoTermsOfService\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etermsOfService | `string` | A URL to the Terms of Service for the API. MUST be in the format of a URL.","\u003ca name=\"infoContact\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003econtact | [Contact Object](#contact-object) | The contact information for the exposed API.","\u003ca name=\"infoLicense\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003elicense | [License Object](#license-object) | The license information for the exposed API.","\u003ca name=\"infoVersion\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eversion | `string` | **REQUIRED**. The version of the OpenAPI document (which is distinct from the [OpenAPI Specification version](#oasVersion) or the API implementation version).","","","This object MAY be extended with [Specification Extensions](#specification-extensions).","","##### Info Object Example:","","```json","{","  \"title\": \"Sample Pet Store App\",","  \"description\": \"This is a sample server for a pet store.\",","  \"termsOfService\": \"http://example.com/terms/\",","  \"contact\": {","    \"name\": \"API Support\",","    \"url\": \"http://www.example.com/support\",","    \"email\": \"support@example.com\"","  },","  \"license\": {","    \"name\": \"Apache 2.0\",","    \"url\": \"https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html\"","  },","  \"version\": \"1.0.1\"","}","```","","```yaml","title: Sample Pet Store App","description: This is a sample server for a pet store.","termsOfService: http://example.com/terms/","contact:","  name: API Support","  url: http://www.example.com/support","  email: support@example.com","license:","  name: Apache 2.0","  url: https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html","version: 1.0.1","```","","#### Contact Object","","Contact information for the exposed API.","","##### Fixed Fields","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"contactName\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ename | `string` | The identifying name of the contact person/organization.","\u003ca name=\"contactUrl\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eurl | `string` | The URL pointing to the contact information. MUST be in the format of a URL.","\u003ca name=\"contactEmail\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eemail | `string` | The email address of the contact person/organization. MUST be in the format of an email address.","","This object MAY be extended with [Specification Extensions](#specification-extensions).","","##### Contact Object Example:","","```json","{","  \"name\": \"API Support\",","  \"url\": \"http://www.example.com/support\",","  \"email\": \"support@example.com\"","}","```","","```yaml","name: API Support","url: http://www.example.com/support","email: support@example.com","```","","#### License Object","","License information for the exposed API.","","##### Fixed Fields","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"licenseName\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ename | `string` | **REQUIRED**. The license name used for the API.","\u003ca name=\"licenseUrl\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eurl | `string` | A URL to the license used for the API. MUST be in the format of a URL.","","This object MAY be extended with [Specification Extensions](#specification-extensions).","","##### License Object Example:","","```json","{","  \"name\": \"Apache 2.0\",","  \"url\": \"https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html\"","}","```","","```yaml","name: Apache 2.0","url: https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html","```","","#### Server Object","","An object representing a Server.","","##### Fixed Fields","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"serverUrl\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eurl | `string` | **REQUIRED**. A URL to the target host.  This URL supports Server Variables and MAY be relative, to indicate that the host location is relative to the location where the OpenAPI document is being served. Variable substitutions will be made when a variable is named in `{`brackets`}`.","\u003ca name=\"serverDescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription | `string` | An optional string describing the host designated by the URL. [CommonMark syntax](http://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.","\u003ca name=\"serverVariables\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003evariables | Map[`string`, [Server Variable Object](#server-variable-object)] | A map between a variable name and its value.  The value is used for substitution in the server's URL template.","","This object MAY be extended with [Specification Extensions](#specification-extensions).","","##### Server Object Example","","A single server would be described as:","","```json","{","  \"url\": \"https://development.gigantic-server.com/v1\",","  \"description\": \"Development server\"","}","```","","```yaml","url: https://development.gigantic-server.com/v1","description: Development server","```","","The following shows how multiple servers can be described, for example, at the OpenAPI Object's [`servers`](#oasServers):","","```json","{","  \"servers\": [","    {","      \"url\": \"https://development.gigantic-server.com/v1\",","      \"description\": \"Development server\"","    },","    {","      \"url\": \"https://staging.gigantic-server.com/v1\",","      \"description\": \"Staging server\"","    },","    {","      \"url\": \"https://api.gigantic-server.com/v1\",","      \"description\": \"Production server\"","    }","  ]","}","```","","```yaml","servers:","- url: https://development.gigantic-server.com/v1","  description: Development server","- url: https://staging.gigantic-server.com/v1","  description: Staging server","- url: https://api.gigantic-server.com/v1","  description: Production server","```","","The following shows how variables can be used for a server configuration:","","```json","{","  \"servers\": [","    {","      \"url\": \"https://{username}.gigantic-server.com:{port}/{basePath}\",","      \"description\": \"The production API server\",","      \"variables\": {","        \"username\": {","          \"default\": \"demo\",","          \"description\": \"this value is assigned by the service provider, in this example `gigantic-server.com`\"","        },","        \"port\": {","          \"enum\": [","            \"8443\",","            \"443\"","          ],","          \"default\": \"8443\"","        },","        \"basePath\": {","          \"default\": \"v2\"","        }","      }","    }","  ]","}","```","","```yaml","servers:","- url: https://{username}.gigantic-server.com:{port}/{basePath}","  description: The production API server","  variables:","    username:","      # note! no enum here means it is an open value","      default: demo","      description: this value is assigned by the service provider, in this example `gigantic-server.com`","    port:","      enum:","        - '8443'","        - '443'","      default: '8443'","    basePath:","      # open meaning there is the opportunity to use special base paths as assigned by the provider, default is `v2`","      default: v2","```","","","#### Server Variable Object","","An object representing a Server Variable for server URL template substitution.","","##### Fixed Fields","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"serverVariableEnum\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eenum | [`string`] | An enumeration of string values to be used if the substitution options are from a limited set.","\u003ca name=\"serverVariableDefault\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edefault | `string` |  **REQUIRED**. The default value to use for substitution, and to send, if an alternate value is _not_ supplied. Unlike the [Schema Object's](#schema-object) `default`, this value MUST be provided by the consumer.","\u003ca name=\"serverVariableDescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription | `string` | An optional description for the server variable. [CommonMark syntax](http://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.","","This object MAY be extended with [Specification Extensions](#specification-extensions).","","#### Components Object","","Holds a set of reusable objects for different aspects of the OAS.","All objects defined within the components object will have no effect on the API unless they are explicitly referenced from properties outside the components object.","","","##### Fixed Fields","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---|---","\u003ca name=\"componentsSchemas\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e schemas | Map[`string`, [Schema Object](#schema-object) \\| [Reference Object](#reference-object)] | An object to hold reusable [Schema Objects](#schema-object).","\u003ca name=\"componentsResponses\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e responses | Map[`string`, [Response Object](#response-object) \\| [Reference Object](#reference-object)] | An object to hold reusable [Response Objects](#response-object).","\u003ca name=\"componentsParameters\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e parameters | Map[`string`, [Parameter Object](#parameter-object) \\| [Reference Object](#reference-object)] | An object to hold reusable [Parameter Objects](#parameter-object).","\u003ca name=\"componentsExamples\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e examples | Map[`string`, [Example Object](#example-object) \\| [Reference Object](#reference-object)] | An object to hold reusable [Example Objects](#example-object).","\u003ca name=\"componentsRequestBodies\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e requestBodies | Map[`string`, [Request Body Object](#request-body-object) \\| [Reference Object](#reference-object)] | An object to hold reusable [Request Body Objects](#request-body-object).","\u003ca name=\"componentsHeaders\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e headers | Map[`string`, [Header Object](#header-object) \\| [Reference Object](#reference-object)] | An object to hold reusable [Header Objects](#header-object).","\u003ca name=\"componentsSecuritySchemes\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e securitySchemes| Map[`string`, [Security Scheme Object](#security-scheme-object) \\| [Reference Object](#reference-object)] | An object to hold reusable [Security Scheme Objects](#security-scheme-object).","\u003ca name=\"componentsLinks\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e links | Map[`string`, [Link Object](#link-object) \\| [Reference Object](#reference-object)] | An object to hold reusable [Link Objects](#link-object).","\u003ca name=\"componentsCallbacks\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e callbacks | Map[`string`, [Callback Object](#callback-object) \\| [Reference Object](#reference-object)] | An object to hold reusable [Callback Objects](#callback-object).","","This object MAY be extended with [Specification Extensions](#specification-extensions).","","All the fixed fields declared above are objects that MUST use keys that match the regular expression: `^[a-zA-Z0-9\\.\\-_]+$`.","","Field Name Examples:","","```","User","User_1","User_Name","user-name","my.org.User","```","","##### Components Object Example","","```json","\"components\": {","  \"schemas\": {","    \"Category\": {","      \"type\": \"object\",","      \"properties\": {","        \"id\": {","          \"type\": \"integer\",","          \"format\": \"int64\"","        },","        \"name\": {","          \"type\": \"string\"","        }","      }","    },","    \"Tag\": {","      \"type\": \"object\",","      \"properties\": {","        \"id\": {","          \"type\": \"integer\",","          \"format\": \"int64\"","        },","        \"name\": {","          \"type\": \"string\"","        }","      }","    }","  },","  \"parameters\": {","    \"skipParam\": {","      \"name\": \"skip\",","      \"in\": \"query\",","      \"description\": \"number of items to skip\",","      \"required\": true,","      \"schema\": {","        \"type\": \"integer\",","        \"format\": \"int32\"","      }","    },","    \"limitParam\": {","      \"name\": \"limit\",","      \"in\": \"query\",","      \"description\": \"max records to return\",","      \"required\": true,","      \"schema\" : {","        \"type\": \"integer\",","        \"format\": \"int32\"","      }","    }","  },","  \"responses\": {","    \"NotFound\": {","      \"description\": \"Entity not found.\"","    },","    \"IllegalInput\": {","      \"description\": \"Illegal input for operation.\"","    },","    \"GeneralError\": {","      \"description\": \"General Error\",","      \"content\": {","        \"application/json\": {","          \"schema\": {","            \"$ref\": \"#/components/schemas/GeneralError\"","          }","        }","      }","    }","  },","  \"securitySchemes\": {","    \"api_key\": {","      \"type\": \"apiKey\",","      \"name\": \"api_key\",","      \"in\": \"header\"","    },","    \"petstore_auth\": {","      \"type\": \"oauth2\",","      \"flows\": {","        \"implicit\": {","          \"authorizationUrl\": \"http://example.org/api/oauth/dialog\",","          \"scopes\": {","            \"write:pets\": \"modify pets in your account\",","            \"read:pets\": \"read your pets\"","          }","        }","      }","    }","  }","}","```","","```yaml","components:","  schemas:","    Category:","      type: object","      properties:","        id:","          type: integer","          format: int64","        name:","          type: string","    Tag:","      type: object","      properties:","        id:","          type: integer","          format: int64","        name:","          type: string","  parameters:","    skipParam:","      name: skip","      in: query","      description: number of items to skip","      required: true","      schema:","        type: integer","        format: int32","    limitParam:","      name: limit","      in: query","      description: max records to return","      required: true","      schema:","        type: integer","        format: int32","  responses:","    NotFound:","      description: Entity not found.","    IllegalInput:","      description: Illegal input for operation.","    GeneralError:","      description: General Error","      content:","        application/json:","          schema:","            $ref: '#/components/schemas/GeneralError'","  securitySchemes:","    api_key:","      type: apiKey","      name: api_key","      in: header","    petstore_auth:","      type: oauth2","      flows:","        implicit:","          authorizationUrl: http://example.org/api/oauth/dialog","          scopes:","            write:pets: modify pets in your account","            read:pets: read your pets","```","","","#### Paths Object","","Holds the relative paths to the individual endpoints and their operations.","The path is appended to the URL from the [`Server Object`](#server-object) in order to construct the full URL.  The Paths MAY be empty, due to [ACL constraints](#security-filtering).","","##### Patterned Fields","","Field Pattern | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"pathsPath\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e/{path} | [Path Item Object](#path-item-object) | A relative path to an individual endpoint. The field name MUST begin with a slash. The path is **appended** (no relative URL resolution) to the expanded URL from the [`Server Object`](#server-object)'s `url` field in order to construct the full URL. [Path templating](#path-templating) is allowed. When matching URLs, concrete (non-templated) paths would be matched before their templated counterparts. Templated paths with the same hierarchy but different templated names MUST NOT exist as they are identical. In case of ambiguous matching, it's up to the tooling to decide which one to use.","","This object MAY be extended with [Specification Extensions](#specification-extensions).","","##### Path Templating Matching","","Assuming the following paths, the concrete definition, `/pets/mine`, will be matched first if used:","","```","  /pets/{petId}","  /pets/mine","```","","The following paths are considered identical and invalid:","","```","  /pets/{petId}","  /pets/{name}","```","","The following may lead to ambiguous resolution:","","```","  /{entity}/me","  /books/{id}","```","","##### Paths Object Example","","```json","{","  \"/pets\": {","    \"get\": {","      \"description\": \"Returns all pets from the system that the user has access to\",","      \"responses\": {","        \"200\": {         ","          \"description\": \"A list of pets.\",","          \"content\": {","            \"application/json\": {","              \"schema\": {","                \"type\": \"array\",","                \"items\": {","                  \"$ref\": \"#/components/schemas/pet\"","                }","              }","            }","          }","        }","      }","    }","  }","}","```","","```yaml","/pets:","  get:","    description: Returns all pets from the system that the user has access to","    responses:","      '200':","        description: A list of pets.","        content:","          application/json:","            schema:","              type: array","              items:","                $ref: '#/components/schemas/pet'","```","","#### Path Item Object","","Describes the operations available on a single path.","A Path Item MAY be empty, due to [ACL constraints](#security-filtering).","The path itself is still exposed to the documentation viewer but they will not know which operations and parameters are available.","","##### Fixed Fields","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"pathItemRef\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e$ref | `string` | Allows for an external definition of this path item. The referenced structure MUST be in the format of a [Path Item Object](#path-item-object). If there are conflicts between the referenced definition and this Path Item's definition, the behavior is *undefined*.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemSummary\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003esummary| `string` | An optional, string summary, intended to apply to all operations in this path.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemDescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription | `string` | An optional, string description, intended to apply to all operations in this path. [CommonMark syntax](http://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemGet\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eget | [Operation Object](#operation-object) | A definition of a GET operation on this path.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemPut\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eput | [Operation Object](#operation-object) | A definition of a PUT operation on this path.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemPost\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003epost | [Operation Object](#operation-object) | A definition of a POST operation on this path.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemDelete\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edelete | [Operation Object](#operation-object) | A definition of a DELETE operation on this path.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemOptions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eoptions | [Operation Object](#operation-object) | A definition of a OPTIONS operation on this path.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemHead\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ehead | [Operation Object](#operation-object) | A definition of a HEAD operation on this path.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemPatch\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003epatch | [Operation Object](#operation-object) | A definition of a PATCH operation on this path.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemTrace\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etrace | [Operation Object](#operation-object) | A definition of a TRACE operation on this path.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemServers\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eservers | [[Server Object](#server-object)] | An alternative `server` array to service all operations in this path.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemParameters\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eparameters | [[Parameter Object](#parameter-object) \\| [Reference Object](#reference-object)] | A list of parameters that are applicable for all the operations described under this path. These parameters can be overridden at the operation level, but cannot be removed there. The list MUST NOT include duplicated parameters. A unique parameter is defined by a combination of a [name](#parameterName) and [location](#parameterIn). The list can use the [Reference Object](#reference-object) to link to parameters that are defined at the [OpenAPI Object's components/parameters](#componentsParameters).","","","This object MAY be extended with [Specification Extensions](#specification-extensions).","","##### Path Item Object Example","","```json","{","  \"get\": {","    \"description\": \"Returns pets based on ID\",","    \"summary\": \"Find pets by ID\",","    \"operationId\": \"getPetsById\",","    \"responses\": {","      \"200\": {","        \"description\": \"pet response\",","        \"content\": {","          \"*/*\": {","            \"schema\": {","              \"type\": \"array\",","              \"items\": {","                \"$ref\": \"#/components/schemas/Pet\"","              }","            }","          }","        }","      },","      \"default\": {","        \"description\": \"error payload\",","        \"content\": {","          \"text/html\": {","            \"schema\": {","              \"$ref\": \"#/components/schemas/ErrorModel\"","            }","          }","        }","      }","    }","  },","  \"parameters\": [","    {","      \"name\": \"id\",","      \"in\": \"path\",","      \"description\": \"ID of pet to use\",","      \"required\": true,","      \"schema\": {","        \"type\": \"array\",","        \"items\": {","          \"type\": \"string\"","        }","      },","      \"style\": \"simple\"","    }","  ]","}","```","","```yaml","get:","  description: Returns pets based on ID","  summary: Find pets by ID","  operationId: getPetsById","  responses:","    '200':","      description: pet response","      content:","        '*/*' :","          schema:","            type: array","            items:","              $ref: '#/components/schemas/Pet'","    default:","      description: error payload","      content:","        'text/html':","          schema:","            $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorModel'","parameters:","- name: id","  in: path","  description: ID of pet to use","  required: true","  schema:","    type: array","    style: simple","    items:","      type: string ","```","","#### Operation Object","","Describes a single API operation on a path.","","##### Fixed Fields","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"operationTags\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etags | [`string`] | A list of tags for API documentation control. Tags can be used for logical grouping of operations by resources or any other qualifier.","\u003ca name=\"operationSummary\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003esummary | `string` | A short summary of what the operation does.","\u003ca name=\"operationDescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription | `string` | A verbose explanation of the operation behavior. [CommonMark syntax](http://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.","\u003ca name=\"operationExternalDocs\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eexternalDocs | [External Documentation Object](#external-documentation-object) | Additional external documentation for this operation.","\u003ca name=\"operationId\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eoperationId | `string` | Unique string used to identify the operation. The id MUST be unique among all operations described in the API. Tools and libraries MAY use the operationId to uniquely identify an operation, therefore, it is RECOMMENDED to follow common programming naming conventions.","\u003ca name=\"operationParameters\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eparameters | [[Parameter Object](#parameter-object) \\| [Reference Object](#reference-object)] | A list of parameters that are applicable for this operation. If a parameter is already defined at the [Path Item](#pathItemParameters), the new definition will override it but can never remove it. The list MUST NOT include duplicated parameters. A unique parameter is defined by a combination of a [name](#parameterName) and [location](#parameterIn). The list can use the [Reference Object](#reference-object) to link to parameters that are defined at the [OpenAPI Object's components/parameters](#componentsParameters).","\u003ca name=\"operationRequestBody\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003erequestBody | [Request Body Object](#request-body-object) \\| [Reference Object](#reference-object) | The request body applicable for this operation.  The `requestBody` is only supported in HTTP methods where the HTTP 1.1 specification [RFC7231](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-4.3.1) has explicitly defined semantics for request bodies.  In other cases where the HTTP spec is vague, `requestBody` SHALL be ignored by consumers.","\u003ca name=\"operationResponses\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eresponses | [Responses Object](#responses-object) | **REQUIRED**. The list of possible responses as they are returned from executing this operation.","\u003ca name=\"operationCallbacks\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ecallbacks | Map[`string`, [Callback Object](#callback-object) \\| [Reference Object](#reference-object)] | A map of possible out-of band callbacks related to the parent operation. The key is a unique identifier for the Callback Object. Each value in the map is a [Callback Object](#callback-object) that describes a request that may be initiated by the API provider and the expected responses. The key value used to identify the callback object is an expression, evaluated at runtime, that identifies a URL to use for the callback operation.","\u003ca name=\"operationDeprecated\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edeprecated | `boolean` | Declares this operation to be deprecated. Consumers SHOULD refrain from usage of the declared operation. Default value is `false`.","\u003ca name=\"operationSecurity\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003esecurity | [[Security Requirement Object](#security-requirement-object)] | A declaration of which security mechanisms can be used for this operation. The list of values includes alternative security requirement objects that can be used. Only one of the security requirement objects need to be satisfied to authorize a request. This definition overrides any declared top-level [`security`](#oasSecurity). To remove a top-level security declaration, an empty array can be used.","\u003ca name=\"operationServers\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eservers | [[Server Object](#server-object)] | An alternative `server` array to service this operation. If an alternative `server` object is specified at the Path Item Object or Root level, it will be overridden by this value.","","This object MAY be extended with [Specification Extensions](#specification-extensions).","","##### Operation Object Example","","```json","{","  \"tags\": [","    \"pet\"","  ],","  \"summary\": \"Updates a pet in the store with form data\",","  \"operationId\": \"updatePetWithForm\",","  \"parameters\": [","    {","      \"name\": \"petId\",","      \"in\": \"path\",","      \"description\": \"ID of pet that needs to be updated\",","      \"required\": true,","      \"schema\": {","        \"type\": \"string\"","      }","    }","  ],","  \"requestBody\": {","    \"content\": {","      \"application/x-www-form-urlencoded\": {","        \"schema\": {","          \"type\": \"object\",","           \"properties\": {","              \"name\": {","                \"description\": \"Updated name of the pet\",","                \"type\": \"string\"","              },","              \"status\": {","                \"description\": \"Updated status of the pet\",","                \"type\": \"string\"","             }","           },","        \"required\": [\"status\"]","        }","      }","    }","  },","  \"responses\": {","    \"200\": {","      \"description\": \"Pet updated.\",","      \"content\": {","        \"application/json\": {},","        \"application/xml\": {}","      }","    },","    \"405\": {","      \"description\": \"Invalid input\",","      \"content\": {","        \"application/json\": {},","        \"application/xml\": {}","      }","    }","  },","  \"security\": [","    {","      \"petstore_auth\": [","        \"write:pets\",","        \"read:pets\"","      ]","    }","  ]","}","```","","```yaml","tags:","- pet","summary: Updates a pet in the store with form data","operationId: updatePetWithForm","parameters:","- name: petId","  in: path","  description: ID of pet that needs to be updated","  required: true","  schema:","    type: string","requestBody:","  content:","    'application/x-www-form-urlencoded':","      schema:","       properties:","          name:","            description: Updated name of the pet","            type: string","          status:","            description: Updated status of the pet","            type: string","       required:","         - status","responses:","  '200':","    description: Pet updated.","    content:","      'application/json': {}","      'application/xml': {}","  '405':","    description: Invalid input","    content:","      'application/json': {}","      'application/xml': {}","security:","- petstore_auth:","  - write:pets","  - read:pets","```","","","#### External Documentation Object","","Allows referencing an external resource for extended documentation.","","##### Fixed Fields","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"externalDocDescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription | `string` | A short description of the target documentation. [CommonMark syntax](http://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.","\u003ca name=\"externalDocUrl\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eurl | `string` | **REQUIRED**. The URL for the target documentation.
